product_name: Visual Timetable for Children at Home
rank_rationale: #1 over #2: When the whole day wobbles — not just one transition — a ten-step home timetable beats a morning-only board because nearly-threes need the full arc visible in one place.
include_rationale: If fridge photos have not stuck, this is the grown-up version — rearrangeable home symbols for shoes, teeth, coat, bag. At nearly three, independence wants a visible plan, not a lecture. Try your own photos first; buy if DIY still fails after a fortnight.

rank_rationale: #2 over #3: When the pain is specifically morning and bedtime — not every transition — a star-progress AM/PM board beats a two-step Now/Next because the child sees the full wake-up or wind-down arc.
include_rationale: Prettier than clinic boards — useful when mornings and bedtimes are the only war zones. Stars give a nearly-three a finished-job feeling fridge photos may lack. Try two photos on the bathroom door before spending.

rank_rationale: #3 over #4: A pocketable Now/Next board wins when one hand-off derails the day — park-to-bath, TV-to-teeth — more than a portable book the child never opens at the door.
include_rationale: When the whole day feels like too much, shrink the horizon: Now shoes, Next park. Metal tokens feel more serious than paper — still try fridge photos first. Buy the board plus only the tokens you will use.

product_name: My Communication Book for Early Years Settings
rating_source: Amazon UK B0CJ5QXG8L (cross-listed on Visual Timetable page)
rank_rationale: #4 over #5: Portable book beats a custom metal strip when the child needs the same symbols in two homes or at a childminder — wall charts do not travel.
include_rationale: Worth it when routines need to travel — childminder days, divorced-household handovers, or a kid who ignores wall charts in rooms they do not own. Heavier than a door strip; skip if one wall chart already works.

rank_rationale: #5 below #4: Custom strip is the budget-precision option when you know exactly which three jobs matter — but it loses to the communication book when symbols must leave the house.
include_rationale: Pick this when a full 35-card kit is overkill — three or four real jobs, printed on metal so it survives the bathroom. Same photos-first rule: blu-tack trial before checkout.
